g.text: Preflight coverage map that explains what update-all will handle and what remains intentionally
update-all updates the common workstation things it can safely detect.
It calls first-party/package-manager commands; it never invents a private updater or silently edits projects.
Secret safety: updater logs are redacted for common token/key shapes, and internal shell calls avoid shell startup
OS packages and package database health
user-local Node runtime when update-node exists
npm itself and globally installed npm CLIs
brew update, tap trust preflight, brew upgrade
manual by design; each repo needs tests and rollback context
manual by design; Hermes/OpenClaw-style stacks should not be surprise-upgraded
manual by design; updating images can break running services
manual by design; environment solves are project-specific
manual by design; downloads are large and workload-specific
never sourced for update logic; do not store raw secrets in .bashrc
installed safe surfaces are updated; project-specific stacks stay explicit
Debian package database integrity check; catches interrupted installs before apt runs.
Ubuntu/Debian system package manager; updates OS packages and security fixes.
safe cache/trash sweep; archives, LLM weights, databases, and project data are left alone
npm funding notices are optional donation links, not errors or bills.
package purpose notes are plain-English guidance; registry/advisory lookups belong to Safety Coach
safe cache/trash sweep; archives, LLM weights, databases, and project data are left alone
Cleanup is interactive in live runs: review each prompt, answer yes only for cleanup you
understand, and use --dry-run to preview commands again.
